CURTIS HOME ST ELIZABETH CENTER, THE

CCN: 075365 · MERIDEN, CT 06450 · Lower Ct River Vly County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesAll Data

Overview

Address
380 CROWN STREET, MERIDEN, CT 06450
Phone
2032374338
Certified beds
60
Avg daily residents
56 (93% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1992-01-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.67 3.12 5.79 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.78 0.55 0.97 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.83 0.69 1.03 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.06 1.88 3.79 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.61 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.03 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.91
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.45
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.84
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
94.1%
annual
RN turnover
93.3%
annual
Administrators departed
New facility — insufficient data.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.10
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.80
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-03-11 14 14 1 84 1 84
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-08-01 12 12 0 48 1 48
Total weighted health score
75.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (29)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

4 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
24 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. E 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. B 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. C 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. B 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-03-11 2025-04-22
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-25
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2023-08-01 2023-10-01
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. C 2021-09-30 2021-11-08
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2021-09-30 2021-11-08
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2021-09-30 2021-11-08
